column3:
Client Notes – Title 8 CCR §334.2Client Overview
  Client Role: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)  Worksite: Prison/Jail Facility – Lancaster, California  Employment Status: Full-time  Schedule: Monday–Friday, 7:00 a.m. – 3:00 p.m.  Hourly Rate: $29/hour  Start Date: April 2025  Prior Employment: Home health agency  Employment Contract: 12-month full employment contract          Renewal Date: July 7, 2025      Job Duties  Works directly with incarcerated patients  Provides support to nursing staff  Frequently on feet during shifts  Periods of prolonged sitting during suicide watch assignmentsApplicable Regulation  Title 8 California Code of Regulations (CCR) §334.2          Workplace safety requirements      Heat exposure and environmental conditions      Employer duty to provide a safe and healthful workplace      Heat Exposure & Environmental Conditions  Facility lacks air conditioning  Client reports extreme heat exposure during shifts  Heat exposure brought to attention of senior management          Manager Notified: Victor (senior management / warehouse)      Management Response: Acknowledged no AC in facility        Potential violations related to:          Excessive heat      Failure to mitigate known hazardous conditions      Break and Meal Period Violations  Only scheduled break is a 30-minute lunch  No 10-minute rest breaks provided  At times, 30-minute lunch is also missed or interrupted  Pattern suggests:          Systemic denial of legally required rest periods      Wage and hour implications in addition to safety concerns      Hazardous Substance Exposure  Exposure to illicit drugs, specifically:          “Spice” being created, smoked, or used by inmates        Exposure occurs within the workplace environment  Raises concerns regarding:          Air quality      Secondhand exposure      Employer failure to control known hazards      Workplace Violence & Harassment  Client reports workplace violence and harassment  Alleged Perpetrator:          Name: Eileen Alvarez      Gender: Female      Approx. Height: 5’5”      Approx. Weight: 180 lbs      Relationship: Co-worker        Nature of conduct: Details to be provided  Potential issues include:          Failure to prevent workplace violence      Failure to investigate or remediate harassment      Retaliation risk      Risk & Liability Considerations (Preliminary)  Known unsafe conditions acknowledged by management  Repeated exposure to extreme heat  Failure to provide required breaks  Exposure to controlled substances in the workplace  Allegations of violence/harassment by a co-worker  Client is under a renewable contract nearing expiration (retaliation timing risk)Next Steps / To Be Added  Detailed facts of workplace violence incident(s)  Any written complaints, emails, or reports made  Witnesses to heat exposure, break violations, or harassment  Medical symptoms or treatment related to heat or exposure  Employer policies on safety, breaks, and workplace violence  Cal/OSHA citation details (if available)Fear for her safety 


Client says violence is repetitive.  The client says co-worker invaded her personal space 3-4 times and made contact with her. She reported all 4 incidents to Supervisor Olisa and Kristen Cooper.


Incident 1 July/2025-Warehouse
-Pushed Trash Cans towards another employee snatched papers out of hands and took pens out of the pockets of her scrubs.


Incident 2 December 2nd  2025 On the Yard


Co worker came up behind her from her left and hip shoved the client pushing to the right and said "I got it " referring to the cart. The client says she was already pushing the cart so there was no reason for her to come up from behind to take the cart. There was a correction office behind them but she is not sure if he saw it. However he was equipped with body cam.


Incident 3 January 14th 2026- Yard inside housing unit behind podium


Inmate was approaching the client with questions, then co-worker approached shoved client with her left shoulder and got in front of her to talk to the inmate. She continued to stand in front of the client. The client told the co-worker not to touch her and the co-worker responded by saying dont talk to me like that. 


Incident 4 January 28, 2026


Client walking to sign out with books. She saw the co-worker standing there in the hall near the sign out book, and before you could approach she said "excuse me". Client stopped walking towards the book and the co-worker then walked straight into her making contact.


She always makes contact with her shoulders and hips. Client does not recall her facial expression. The co-worker has not made any verbal threats. The client says she is afraid because it could escalate due to the repettition. She reiterates that she reported the first 3 incidents and the most were caught on surveillance. 


When she reported the issue to Hooper for December through January, she is told that the videos do not establish intent or could be validated. Hooper is the Chief Executive Nurse. They continue to have the client work with the same co-worker since July.
Letter of Representation and Demand Letter sent by certified mail to Defendant(s), on 01/31/2026

column2: Copy of Settlement Invitation Sent Via First Class Mail
column3: Copy of invitation to settle sent to plaintiff counsel via first class mail.

Deceased: Female /137lbs/5'2
DOB: 11/30/1987
Date of Death: 01/03/2023
Cause of Action Filed: 12/19/2024
Place of Death: Loma Linda University Hospital, approximately 3:11 pm
Admitted: Around 8:00 am, 01/03/2023
Deceased No Children/Finace/Living Parents/No Siblings
Running Hamburger stand and a hair business with Mother, and involved in her community.
No Prexisting Medical Conditions
No Known Allergies Co-Morbidities
No Drug or Alcohol Use
Coroner Rules: Natural Cause of Death
Autopsy San Bernardino County
Facts:
On December 25th, the deceased complained of her mouth hurting due to an abscess. She went to Western Dental on the 29th for care and received a deep cleaning. After the deep cleaning, she bled profusely for days and was in so much pain that she developed lockjaw and could not speak. On the 3rd, she proceeded to the hospital via paramedics, and upon arrival, tests were done, and it was determined that she had low hemoglobin counts, so a transfusion was ordered.
She was placed on a ventilator. However, by the time that they could administer the blood transfusion, she had coded and thus died. Western Dental was notified that she had passed on around February of the same year. And we don't know if the pathologist knew the abscess or the hemoglobin issues, but we do know that the fiancé is the one who accompanied her via ambulance to Loma Linda, and yeah, she just essentially passed away. And the persons that could attest that saw the swelling and the bleeding would be her father, Ronald Perrywell, her fiancé Doug, and her friend Nadia Vann, who had accompanied her to the appointment at Western Dental.

Name: Ronald Frank Pennywell
Phone: 213.334 5242
Address: 12003 South Main Street Apt 313, Los Angeles, CA 90061
DOB: 02/27/1964
Father of the Decedent:


Decedent had been residing with both of her parents and was in the process of moving to the San Bernardino area.


She kept telling him that her mouth was hurting, and she eventually made an appointment to go to the Dentist. He didn't think too much about it. He just knows that she was complaining about pain to the point that she was not eating, even at Christmas dinne,r she did not eat and actually brought him food.


She was bleeding when she returned from the Dentist with a bleeding mouth. So she told him that the Dentist gave her an (numbing) injection. 


The night before she left to go to San Bernardino, she did not have much to say or do with anyone, not even her dog (Leak)  that she adored so much.;

Name: Nydia Vanns
Phone: 562.479.9567
Email: VannsNydia2@gmail.com
Address: 113 East Cypress Street Apt 1 Compton, CA 90220
DOB: 07/14/1981
Relation to Decedent: Bestfriends (8 years)

Pennywell and Vanns worked together and had the same work schedule on the majority of the days. They even spent time together on their off days. They actually spoke every single day of the week.
12/25
Vanns spoke to Pennywell on Christmas Day and the Day after Christmas. They wished eachother Merry Christmas. Pennywell told her that she was not feeling to good because her mouth was in pain. They only talked for about 10 minutes that day when they usually speak for hours.

12/27
They talked again Vanns asked her if she wanted to go out. She said she did not feel well and said perhaps she would Vanns tomorrow if she felt better.

12/28
Vanns' stepson was murdered on this day, and Pennywell spent the whole day and evening with her. They departed about 8:30 pm. She says Pennywell kept complaining and did not eat like they normally do. She says Pennywell is an "Eater" and they did not eat, so she knew that her mouth was hurting.

12/29
Pennywell says she is going to the Dentist because she was in so much pain and her face was swollen.
They went to the Dentist and her mouth was swollen; she does not recall which side, but knows that it was definitely swollen. Vanns says the swelling was visibly noticeable,e and you could tell by the way that she was talking. She was not trying to open her mouth or move it too much. She did not go inside the dental room because she was not allowed.

After over an hour, Pennywell comes out of the room to the lobby. She was bleeding from the mouth really badly and kept spitting out blood. They drove from the dentist to their job at Vons in Pacific Palisades and got her cup so that she could rinse her moouth out. Vanns also got her another cup of water but she could not drink because it was so much blood. She was bleeding all the way back home spitting up blood the entire time.

Vanns said she asked Pennywell if she should keep bleeding like this, and she said she doesn't know. Pennywell says they told her that she would bleed just bit. Profuse bleeding.

Pennywell says  I am going to go to Dad's, rest, and get on the road to Redlands. But when Vanns called, she said in a muffled distored voice that she never left because she was still bleeding and felt sick.

The last time she saw Pennywell was the day they went to the Dentist. They exchanged New Year's messages, and she did not hear from her for days. She sent a text message, but she never responded. She learned of her death from Pennywell's sister. She says her sister says she is gone. She was rushed to the hospital for a blood transfusion, and she is gone. Vanns felt sick like she had to throw up. She could not believe that her bestfriend was gone.

Vanns says she was shocked because her best friend was relatively healthy. In fact, when she got the call that they were rushing her to the hospital, she thought it was a joke.

She confirmed that no prescription was issued or aftercare instructions. They did not send her home with anything. She did not mention that any tests or cultures were run.

I received a return call from Jaylun S in the complaints unit of the DCA. He searched their records and could not locate a complaint or report of any fatality in connection with the Decedent reported by the provider or Western Dental. I spoke to him on 02 6 26 at 12:10 p.m.

To date the opposing party has not filed an answer to our complaint and we are now in active default.
Next steps:
File statement of Damages
Reach out to Court Regarding the Motion on the Second Amended Complaint

On February 26, 2026, at approximately 5:30 p.m., I spoke telephonically with defense counsel, Mr. Poliquin, regarding the upcoming conference scheduled for Tuesday, March 3, 2026. During that call, we discussed Plaintiff’s anticipated request for a continuance. Mr. Poliquin indicated Defendants’ position that two newly added parties were improperly included and stated that he likely intends to file a demurrer. He inquired whether Plaintiff would dismiss the parent company and the individual doctor; I advised that he may proceed with any responsive pleading he deems appropriate. Mr. Poliquin also asked whether Plaintiff had a settlement demand. I informed him I would confer with my client.

After conferring with Plaintiff regarding valuation, including discussion of MICRA caps and applicable statutory limitations, Plaintiff authorized a settlement demand of $7,000,000.

column2: Settlment Offer Presented to Defense Counsel
column3: On February 27, 2026, at approximately 1:31 p.m., I telephonically communicated the $7,000,000 settlement demand to Mr. Poliquin. He stated he would present the demand to his clients. The call concluded thereafter.
Request for Public Records was sent to CDPH P028892-030226
Text sent to Client advised them that opposing counsel was open to negotiations. The client asked me to offer them 10K settlement offer.
